charts.title: Bar (vertical)
charts.ariaLabel: Vertical bar chart example showing visits by channel.
charts.description: The default choice for comparing value across categories. Switch to horizontal bars if category labels are long.

charts.title: Bar (horizontal)
charts.ariaLabel: Horizontal bar chart example showing requests by region.
charts.description: Use when showing a ranked list, or when category labels are too long to read on a vertical chart.

charts.title: Stacked bar
charts.ariaLabel: Stacked bar chart example showing completion status by program.
charts.description: Shows the composition of a whole across categories, making it easy to spot differences in the mix. Keep segments to three or four and label clearly.

charts.title: Grouped bar
charts.ariaLabel: Grouped horizontal bar chart comparing request counts by region across two years.
charts.description: Compares two or more series side by side within each category, making it easy to see differences between series at a glance.

charts.title: Line
charts.ariaLabel: Line chart example showing applications over time.
charts.description: The standard choice for showing change over a continuous time period. Use multi-line when you need to compare more than one series.

charts.title: Multi-line
charts.ariaLabel: Multi-line chart example showing two monthly trends over time.
charts.description: Compares how multiple series trend over the same time period. Works best with three or four series. Beyond that, patterns become difficult to separate.

charts.title: Area
charts.ariaLabel: Area chart example showing total users over time.
charts.description: Shows cumulative volume or share building over time. Use sparingly as a line chart is usually clearer for non-cumulative data.

charts.title: Lines + bar
charts.ariaLabel: Combined line and bar chart showing application volume and conversion rate by month.
charts.description: Shows two related measures with different units over the same period. Only use this when the relationship between the two measures is the point of the chart.

charts.title: Stacked area
charts.ariaLabel: Stacked area chart example showing cumulative channel share over time.
charts.description: Shows how components contribute to a changing total over time. Better than a multi-line chart when the cumulative total is as important as the individual series.

charts.title: Doughnut
charts.ariaLabel: Doughnut chart example showing approval status with direct slice labels.
charts.description: Shows simple proportion between two to four segments. The centre space can display a total or key callout value, which makes it a common choice for dashboards. Use a bar chart when precise comparison between segments matters more than showing the whole.

charts.title: Pie
charts.ariaLabel: Pie chart example showing channel share with direct slice labels.
charts.description: Functionally identical to a doughnut but without the centre space. Doughnuts are generally preferred in digital products as the centre can carry additional context.

charts.title: Stacked proportional bar (100%)
charts.ariaLabel: 100 percent stacked bar chart showing proportional outcome share by channel.
charts.description: Compares proportional composition across categories where each bar sums to 100%. Because actual counts are hidden, provide the absolute total for each category so users can judge the scale behind the percentages.

charts.title: Scatter plot
charts.ariaLabel: Scatter plot example showing relationship between two variables.
charts.description: Shows the relationship or correlation between two variables across a dataset. Use only when there is a genuine relationship worth showing.

charts.title: Bubble plot
charts.ariaLabel: Bubble plot example showing demand and wait time across locations with bubble size as volume.
charts.description: Shows relationship and magnitude across two variables, with bubble size encoding a third dimension. Use only when all three dimensions are meaningful, otherwise a scatter plot is simpler and clearer.

charts.title: Vertical dot plot
charts.ariaLabel: Vertical dot plot example showing distribution of wait times by weekday.
charts.description: Shows distribution across categories with less visual weight than bars. A good alternative to a bar chart when showing the spread of individual data points matters as much as the category totals.

charts.title: Histogram
charts.ariaLabel: Histogram example showing distribution of processing times in binned ranges.
charts.description: Shows how values are distributed across a continuous numeric range. Use this instead of a bar chart when the data is continuous and the shape of the distribution is the story.

charts.title: Radar
charts.ariaLabel: Radar chart example comparing capability scores across dimensions.
charts.description: Compares profiles across the same fixed set of dimensions. Use only when the overall shape of the profile matters, as a grouped bar chart or table is clearer when precise values are the priority.
